hypothesis: For multi-component plasma mixtures containing three or more gases, augmenting an inductive GNN that aggregates pre-trained single-gas hidden-layer embeddings with physics-informed pairwise edge features (e.g., counts of shared reaction pathways, representative reaction rate coefficients, or differences in ionization potentials) will improve mixture etch-rate prediction accuracy, yielding a statistically significant increase in test-set R2 of at least 0.05 relative to the same GNN architecture using only scalar composition-weighted node features.
